data.title: Oakmark Equity And Income Fund exits BlackRock, adds Mondelez in Q4
data.description:
• Oakmark Equity and Income Fund outperformed the benchmark S&P 500 and Bloomberg U.S. Aggregate Bond Index during the fourth quarter.
  • The largest contributors to the performance were communication services and health care, while industrials were detractors.
  • The quarterly performance was marked by a market rally, with many value and smaller capitalization stocks performing well.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Amrize (AMRZ), Gartner (IT), and Mondelez International (MDLZ).
  • The fund exited BlackRock (BLK) and Charter Communications (CHTR).
  • Source: Fund Letter

data.title: SA Asks: What are the most attractive REIT stocks right now?
data.description:
What are the most attractive REIT stocks for investors right now?

Seeking Alpha analysts Ivo Kolchev and IWA Research weigh in.

Ivo Kolchev: When it comes to REITs, I advise readers to diversify across cyclical sectors (such as hotels and retail) and defensive sectors (such as healthcare and residential). Obviously, the exact mix will depend on individual investor circumstances, but being prepared for downside surprises in economic growth is pertinent. I also expect REIT M&A to pick up in 2027; hence, I would give a preference to smaller REITs that have a chance of being acquired.

For U.S. markets, hotel REITs such as RLJ Lodging Trust (RLJ) look particularly attractive in light of an expected rebound in U.S. GDP growth and a packed 2026 event calendar, most notably the FIFA World Cup. Some other cyclical REITs to consider would be CBL & Associates Properties (CBL) and Orion Properties (ONL), which offer attractive valuations but also carry a lot of debt, which is a risk to consider. Defensive REIT picks would include healthcare REITs such as Community Healthcare Trust (CHCT) and Medical Properties Trust (MPW).

For Europe, I would highlight German residential players such as Vonovia (VONOY) and LEG Immobilien (LEGIF), which offer mid-single-digit dividends and ample capital gain potential as they trade well below book value, which is expected to rise in 2026. Some smaller cyclical names would include retail REITs such as Wereldhave Belgium (WRDEF) and Immobiliare Grande Distribuzione (IMMQF), which trade at cap rates of about 8% to 9% despite solid occupancy of about 95%.

IWA Research: My pick would be RLJ Lodging Trust (RLJ), a REIT that owns premium-branded hotels across the U.S., currently paying a nearly 8% dividend yield. Despite the stock's weakness in recent years that I believe is generated by industry-wide macro pressure, the company stands to benefit significantly in 2026 from unique factors such as the World Cup and America 250, while still trading at significantly undervalued levels. They are also in a very strong financial position for a REIT, with significant cash on hand, manageable leverage, and a PB of around 0.52, while still generating a solid AFFO.

  • Top Hotel & Resort REIT Stocks
  • Top Retail REIT Stocks
  • Top Office REIT Stocks
  • Top Healthcare REIT Stocks
  • Top Real Estate Operating Company Stocks

data.title: PennantPark Investment estimates NII per share at $0.13-$0.15 for FQ1
data.description:
PennantPark Investment (PNNT) estimated its core net investment income for the fiscal first quarter ended December 31, 2025, between $0.13 and $0.15 per share, vs. $0.15 in the prior quarter.

Net investment income is estimated to be between $0.10 and $0.12 per share, compared to $0.15 per share in the prior quarter.

Net asset value per share is estimated to be between $6.97 and $7.02 per share at December 31, 2025, compared to $7.11 at September 30, 2025.

data.title: LVMH slides in Paris trading after Morgan Stanley pulls its bull rating
data.description:
Morgan Stanley downgraded LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton (LVMHF) (LVMUY) to an Equal-weight rating on Monday after having the stock set at Overweight.

Analyst Edouard Aubin and his team see greater downside than upside risk to EPS consensus numbers for 2026 due largely to FX and tariff headwinds, as well as other issues such as the wines & spirits division's top line likely remaining under pressure.

Aubin warned that LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) will be only partially able to offset the FX and tariff headwinds through price increases given the necessity to not price out the middle-income consumer. As for the wine & spirits business, Aubin noted that industry data points to a number of challenges intensifying, such as changing consumer behaviors, competition from other alcohol players, and increased tariffs in the key U.S. and Chinese markets. "We now model that the W&S division's profit will fall to €988m (18.1% margin) in 2026, the lowest level since 2010," wrote Aubin.

The overall takeaway is that the stock is unlikely to continue to appreciate in the coming weeks.

Morgan Stanley set a price target of €635 on LVMH (LVMHF).

LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) operates six main business lines that don't always see the same peaks and valleys in terms of demand and sales. The Paris-based company's business lines are wines and spirits, fashion and leather goods, perfumes and cosmetics, watches and jewelry, selective retailing (notably Sephora and travel retail), and other activities including hospitality and media.

Shares of LVMH (LVMHF) (LVMUY) fell 4.1% in Paris trading after the ratings slash from Morgan Stanley.

data.title: OTTR, HE draw highest short interest in utilities sector; BIP and NEE see lowest short interest
data.description:
Within the utilities sector, short sellers remain heavily concentrated in electric utilities, with several electricity producers and multi-utility companies ranking among the most heavily shorted stocks in the market.

Otter Tail (OTTR) and Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) recorded the highest short interest among utilities stocks with a market capitalization of over $2B. 

The utilities sector (XLU) comprises companies involved in services like electricity, natural gas, and water, among other services.

Otter Tail (OTTR) was the most shorted stock in the utilities sector, with short interest at 11.91% of its shares outstanding, followed by Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) at 11.29%, reflecting elevated bearish positioning in both stocks.

Seeking Alpha Quant ratings and analysts are cautious and have rated OTTR as a Hold, while Wall Street analysts are bullish and have rated it a Buy.

Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) was rated a Hold by Seeking Alpha Quant ratings and Wall Street analysts. However, Seeking Alpha analysts rated the stock a Buy.

At the other end of the spectrum, Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP) and NextEra Energy (NEE) were among the least shorted utilities stocks, with short interest of 0.21% and 1.55%, respectively.

The top five most shorted utilities stocks with a market capitalization above $2B (as a percentage of shares outstanding) are:

  • Otter Tail (OTTR) 11.91% short interest
  • Hawaiian Electric Industries (HE) 11.29%
  • Oklo (OKLO) 10.86%
  • IDACORP (IDA) 8.13%
  • Alliant Energy (LNT) 7.60%

The five least shorted utilities stocks, with a market capitalization above $2B, are:

  • Public Service Enterprise Group (PEG) 1.62% short interest
  • California Water Service Group (CWT) 1.61%
  • Eversource Energy (ES) 1.60%
  • NextEra Energy (NEE) 1.55%
  • Brookfield Infrastructure Partners (BIP) 0.21%

data.title: Earnings week ahead: Netflix takes the spotlight, but these reports are also on watch
data.description:
After the big banks officially kicked off the season, the focus this week shifts toward earnings across several sectors, including tech, healthcare, energy, and industrial giants. While U.S. markets are closed on Monday, the rest of the week is packed with earnings.

Notable players on the watchlist:

Monday, January 19

The week opens quietly as U.S. exchanges are closed in observance of Martin Luther King Jr. Day.

Tuesday, January 20

Netflix (NFLX) 

Netflix (NFLX) is set to report its Q4 results after the market closes on Tuesday. Investors will be watching continued subscriber momentum and spending figures, as well as any commentary on its contentious mega merger with Warner Bros. (WBD). Note that Netflix (NFLX) shares underwent a 10-for-1 split in November 2025.

"Recent merger speculation with Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) has pressured NFLX’s share price, but core operations remain resilient and cash flow positive," SA Investing Group Leader Grant Gigliotti writes in Netflix And Its Real Value.

Consensus EPS Estimate: $0.55

Consensus Revenue Estimate: $11.97B

Earnings Insight: The company has exceeded EPS expectations in 6 of the past 8 quarters.

Also reporting: 3M (MMM), United Airlines (UAL), Interactive Brokers (IBKR), and D.R. Horton (DHI).

Wednesday, January 21

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) 

Some recent news out of J&J (JNJ) includes entering a pact with the Trump administration for drug discounts and presenting at the annual J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.

"Shares are up by more than 50% in the past 12 months, making it among the leading DJIA stocks in the last year. But I'm downgrading shares, purely on valuation," SA analyst Mike Zaccardi writes in Johnson & Johnson: Tempering Expectations After A Massive 2025.

Consensus EPS Estimate: $2.47

Consensus Revenue Estimate: $24.16B

Also reporting: Truist Financial (TFC), Kinder Morgan (KMI), and Prologis (PLD).

Thursday, January 22

GE Aerospace (GE)

GE Aerospace (GE) will unveil its quarterly results before the market opens on Thursday. As a pure-play aviation company and one of the pillars of the commercial engine market, pay attention to guidance on delivery targets, which remains the primary driver of the company's long-term valuation. 

GE shares are up over 80% over the past year. See all the recent Seeking Alpha news on GE

Consensus EPS Estimate: $1.43

Consensus Revenue Estimate: $11.20B

Also reporting: Procter & Gamble (PG), Intel (INTC), Abbott (ABT), and CSX (CSX).

Friday, January 23

SLB (SLB)

SLB (SLB) (once known as Schlumberger) is set to disclose results before Friday's open. As one of the largest names in oilfield services, its report will serve as an important barometer for the energy market.

"Venezuelan oil can revitalize oilfield services growth," writes SA analyst Michael Del Monte, though Dair Sansyzbayev cautions that "excessive optimism is already baked in before earnings." 

Consensus EPS Estimate: $0.74

Consensus Revenue Estimate: $9.54B

Also reporting: Ericsson (ERIC) and Booz Allen Hamilton (BAH).
